fields.description:
# Add CI as a second Gerrit gate vote (`Verified`) via gerrit-to-platform â†’ GitHub Actions

## Context
The v1 code gate (epics d251 infra + b744 code-review, both closed) uses a SINGLE
deterministic vote: the Gerrit `LLM-Review` label, cast by the review-bot's four-pass
blocker. The intended end state requires BOTH (a) LLM review AND (b) CI (build / test /
lint / typecheck) to pass. This epic adds the second, independent vote â€” the Gerrit
`Verified` label â€” cast by CI.

**This runs against the LIVE Gerrit-gatekeeper topology** (Gerrit owns `main`; GitHub is a
locked, read-only mirror). It is a **proof-of-concept** that mechanically proves the
review/signing/gate flow with a real, independent CI vote â€” used by 1â€“2 developers on one
project, with agentic development producing frequent commits.

### Decision log (2026-07-01 brainstorm â€” grounded in prior art)
- **Runner = GitHub Actions**, reusing the existing `.github/workflows/test.yml` checks. The
  rebar GitHub repo is **public**, so Actions is free (incl. the macOS matrix cell) and the
  run page is a **zero-auth, contributor-viewable log surface** â€” satisfying the
  "clients can view logs" requirement at no cost.
- **Integration = adopt `gerrit-to-platform` (g2p) WHOLESALE**, the Linux Foundation
  RelEng Gerritâ†’GitHub-Actions bridge, exactly as ONAP / O-RAN-SC / OpenDaylight / FD.io
  deploy it (~96 repos carry a `gerrit-verify.yaml`). This is the only mature, production-
  proven pattern for this integration. A bespoke hybrid was **explicitly rejected** as a
  Frankenstein that mirrors no real Gerrit deployment. (Jenkins+Checks and Zuul are equally
  faithful patterns but presuppose a standing CI fleet whose value is multi-repo speculative
  gating â€” scale we do not have; both were rejected as over-weight for one repo.)
- **The CI `Verified` vote is an authenticated Gerrit label vote** (service account over SSH),
  NOT a per-vote HMAC attestation. The "signing flow" is already carried by the existing
  attestation infra; this epic just adds the independent second gate vote.
- **Two entailed posture changes accepted** (both are the g2p production standard; the
  underlying ADRs are reconsiderable):
  1. **Broaden Gerrit replication to mirror change refs** to the GitHub repo â€” a deliberate
     **reversal of ADR-0010** (main+tags-only mirror). **NOT a wildcard `refs/*`** (that would
     replicate `refs/meta/config`, which embeds the webhook token â€” ADR-0010/0014 keep it off
     GitHub): use a **scoped refspec** `refs/changes/*:refs/changes/*` added alongside the
     existing lines, `replicatePermissions = false` retained, `refs/meta/*` excluded.
     `checkout-gerrit-change-action`'s `gerrit-url` fallback covers replication lag.
  2. **Inbound GitHubâ†’Gerrit SSH vote path** â€” a Gerrit service account whose private key is a
     GitHub Actions secret; the workflow SSHes into Gerrit (port 29418) to cast `Verified`.

### Durable decisions to record as ADRs (not just this prose log)
- **ADR: g2p adoption** as the Gerritâ†’GitHub-Actions CI bridge (topology + trust model).
- **ADR: inbound GitHubâ†’Gerrit SSH vote path** (blast radius, key scope, rotation).
- **ADR: supersede/amend ADR-0010** (replication now includes `refs/changes/*`).
- **ADR: supersede/amend ADR-0013** (the gate is now two independent votes, not one).

## Locked design (the g2p production pattern)
- **Trigger:** g2p Gerrit `hooks` (`patchset-created` / `comment-added` / `change-merged`)
  `workflow_dispatch` a `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` with the nine `GERRIT_*`
  inputs. (Single repo â†’ no org `.github` "magic repo"; a local, non-`required` workflow.)
- **Runtime placement (resolve in S3):** the `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` image is
  **Java-only (no Python)**; g2p needs Python â‰Ą3.11. Decide and document ONE: (a) run g2p on
  the **host**, invoked via the bind-mounted `$site/hooks/` dir (host Python), or (b) add a
  Python layer to the Gerrit image / a sidecar. Preference: host-side to avoid mutating the
  upstream image â€” to be verified.
- **Workflow = fixed 3-job shape**, all actions SHA-pinned:
  1. `clear-vote` â†’ `gerrit-review-action vote-type: clear` (resets `Verifiedâ†’0`; the
     GerriScary-safe reset), then a short `sleep` to allow replication.
  2. `build-and-test` â†’ `checkout-gerrit-change-action` (fetch the exact `GERRIT_REFSPEC`)
     then **our existing `test.yml` steps** (lint / typecheck / test matrix incl. macOS +
     module-size / prompt-index / criteria-routing gates + coverage floor).
  3. `vote` (`if: always()`) â†’ `im-open/workflow-conclusion` then `gerrit-review-action
     vote-type: ${{ WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION }}` â†’ `Verified` +1 (success) / -1 (failure|cancelled),
     with the **run URL in the vote message** (the log link).
- **Gate:** new `[label "Verified"]` (NoBlock, Â±1, `copyCondition = changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE`
  mirroring `LLM-Review`'s strict reset) + `[submit-requirement "Verified"] submittableIf =
  label:Verified=MAX`; voting restricted to Service Users. Final gate:
  `label:LLM-Review=MAX AND label:Verified=MAX AND -has:unresolved`.
- **Re-run policy:** g2p `recheck` ChatOps (`comment-added` mapping `recheck â†’ verify`);
  a new patchset auto-cancels the prior run (`concurrency: cancel-in-progress`).
- **Fail-closed:** any dropped hook / failed run / crashed bridge yields no `+1` â‡’ no submit
  (safe block, never a false pass) â€” but see Monitoring: fail-closed must be a *surfaced*
  block, not silence.

### GerriScary-safe non-negotiables (CVE-2025-1568)
A carried/stale CI vote on an attacker's new patchset is a false-pass even when fail-closed.
Required, together: revision/patchset-scoped vote (the tested refspec) + `copyCondition` that
resets `Verified` on any real code change + `clear-vote` reset at run start +
`Verified` votable only by the CI service account + submit requirement keyed on the current
patchset.

## Success Criteria
- A second, independent gate vote â€” the Gerrit `Verified` label â€” is cast by GitHub Actions
  (via g2p) on every patchset.
- The submit requirement requires BOTH votes: a change cannot submit unless LLM review passes
  AND CI passes; the two are independent.
- CI runs the SAME checks as `test.yml`, and contributors can view each run's logs (the public
  Actions run page, linked from the vote).
- The whole flow is proven end-to-end on the live Gerrit: red CI blocks submit; `recheck`
  re-runs; a green change with both votes submits and replicates to GitHub `main`.
- Operators are alerted on g2p dispatch / vote failures; there is a tested back-out.

## Secrets & credentials (infra invariant â€” no plaintext)
Every credential this epic introduces MUST follow the established pattern: **SSM SecureString
+ materialize-at-boot** (as with `gerrit-bot-token`, `github-replication-deploy-key`, the
GitHub OAuth secret). Applies to the **g2p org PAT** (Actions r/w, Contents+Metadata r) and
the **CI service-account SSH key**. No plaintext PAT in `gerrit_to_platform.ini` on disk;
inject at boot via the existing `fetch-secrets.sh` path. Define a **rotation + retire** path
for both the PAT and `GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY` (compromise response + PoC decommission).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **S1 â€” Gate config** (`infra/gerrit/project.config`): add `[label "Verified"]` (NoBlock,
      Â±1, `copyCondition = changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE`) + `[submit-requirement "Verified"]
      submittableIf = label:Verified=MAX`; grant the CI service account's group
      `label-Verified -1..+1`. **Activate the two-vote submit requirement LAST** (after S3â€“S5
      prove the voter works) to avoid blocking all submits during rollout. Update
      `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` (gate section) and supersede/amend **ADR-0013**.
- [ ] **S2 â€” Replication broadening** + superseding ADR: add a **scoped** `refs/changes/*`
      refspec (NOT `refs/*`) to `infra/gerrit/replication.config`, keeping
      `replicatePermissions = false` and excluding `refs/meta/*`; update the refspec table in
      `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md`; write the ADR that supersedes/amends **ADR-0010** and confirm
      the mirror-lock ruleset still bars non-replication writes to `main`.
- [ ] **S3 â€” g2p install** on the Gerrit host: install + **sha256-pin** the `hooks` plugin via
      `infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh` (verify its bundle status in `gerrit:3.14.1`; update
      `plugins/README.md`); **resolve the Python-runtime placement** (host vs image/sidecar,
      per Runtime placement above); `pip install gerrit-to-platform` (Pyâ‰Ą3.11); symlink the
      three console-script hooks into `$site/hooks/`; write `gerrit_to_platform.ini` (PAT via
      SSM, per Secrets) + `replication.config` symlink + `recheck â†’ verify` mapping.
      **Coexistence proof (concrete):** push a test patchset and observe BOTH the review-bot
      webhook `LLM-Review` vote AND the g2p hook `workflow_dispatch` fire on the same event.
- [ ] **S4 â€” Service account + credentials:** provision a CI Gerrit service account using
      `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` as the template (account + group + `Verified` ACL); store
      the SSH key + PAT via **SSM** (per Secrets); set GitHub repo vars `GERRIT_SERVER` /
      `GERRIT_SSH_USER` / `GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS` / `GERRIT_URL` and secret `GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY`;
      document the inbound SSH trust path + key blast-radius + rotation/retire.
- [ ] **S5 â€” The workflow** `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`: the 3-job shape
      (`clear-vote` â†’ `build-and-test` reusing `test.yml` steps via `checkout-gerrit-change-action`
      â†’ `vote` off `WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION`), all actions SHA-pinned, run URL in the vote message.
      **Verify the action interfaces first** â€” confirm `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action`'s
      actual `vote-type` input vocabulary (incl. `clear` semantics: reset-to-0 vs remove) and
      `checkout-gerrit-change-action`'s inputs against their published `action.yml` before wiring.
      Decide test.yml convergence (recommend the `gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml` hybrid so
      one file serves Gerrit dispatch + manual/branch runs).
- [ ] **S6 â€” Monitoring, back-out, E2E proof + docs:** add a **CloudWatch alarm** for g2p
      hook-dispatch failures and `Verified`-vote failures (model on `monitoring_s4b.tf`'s
      `voter_errors`); document + test a **back-out** (disable the `Verified` submit requirement
      to restore single-vote gating if g2p/CI is broken). On the live Gerrit, prove a red CI
      blocks submit, `recheck` re-runs, and a green change with both votes submits + replicates;
      update `CONTRIBUTING.md` (two-vote flow + `recheck`); capture design notes (cost / latency
      budget, flaky-test handling, `copyCondition` re-run behavior). Record the change URL + votes.

## Deployment sequencing & risk (from plan-review remediation)
- **Riskiest assumptions to spike FIRST** (before building on them): (1) `hooks` plugin
  availability/enablement on the Gerrit host; (2) Python-runtime placement for g2p; (3)
  `gerrit-review-action` `vote-type` vocabulary incl. `clear`.
- **Ordering:** S2 (replication) + `hooks` plugin (S3) â†’ S3/S4 (g2p + creds) â†’ S5 (workflow) â†’
  prove the voter green â†’ **only then** S1 activates the two-vote submit requirement (S6 E2E).
  This closes the "gate enforced but no voter exists" window (all submits blocked).
- **Failure surfacing:** g2p hookâ†’`workflow_dispatch` failures (network / rate-limit / bad PAT)
  must alert (S6 monitoring), not silently leave a change unvotable.

## Decomposition
Six stories (S1â€“S6 above). S2 + the `hooks` plugin are the earliest config/infra; S3 depends on
S2 + `hooks`; S4 pairs with S3; S5 depends on S4 (creds); S6 (monitoring + back-out + E2E) is
gated on S1â€“S5, and S1's submit-requirement activation is deliberately last.

## Out of scope
- The `LLM-Review` vote itself (v1; epics d251 / b744).
- Per-vote HMAC signing of the CI verdict (decided: authenticated label vote only).
- Jenkins / Zuul self-hosted CI (rejected: over-weight for a single repo).
- GitLab support in g2p (not yet available upstream).

## Key references
- g2p: github.com/lfit/releng-gerrit_to_platform (README mandates full mirroring of change
  refs); docs.releng.linuxfoundation.org/projects/gerrit-to-platform.
- Real workflows: onap/.github + o-ran-sc/.github `gerrit-required-verify.yaml`; the annotated
  single-repo template `lfit/releng-gerrit_to_platform/examples/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`.
- Companion actions (SHA-pin, verify action.yml): `lfreleng-actions/checkout-gerrit-change-action`,
  `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action`, `im-open/workflow-conclusion`.
- GerriScary / CVE-2025-1568 (stale-vote supply-chain race) â€” the copyCondition + reset rationale.
- Local: `infra/gerrit/project.config`, `infra/gerrit/replication.config`,
  `infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh`, `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh`, `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md`,
  `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md`, `monitoring_s4b.tf` (alarm template), ADR-0007 (webhook receiver),
  ADR-0010 (replication), ADR-0013 (LLM-Review label), ADR-0014 (token not replicated),
  `.github/workflows/test.yml`.
